scenes.synopsis: MARA hauls the last crates as the harbor lights flicker and die — the whole coast goes dark.
scenes.scriptText:
EXT. HARBOR BREAKWATER - DUSK

MARA CHEN, 24, weather-beaten, drags crates up the slick concrete. The string of harbor lights STUTTERS — then the entire coastline blacks out at once.

MARA
That's not a fuse.

elements.name: Coastline blackout
elements.description: Kill city glow on the far shore

scenes.synopsis: ELI fights the failing transmitter by torchlight; the backup generator coughs and holds.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. COASTAL RADIO STATION - NIGHT

ELI OKORO, 39, sweeps a torch over a wall of dead meters. He slaps the generator. It COUGHS, catches, and one green light holds.

ELI
Come on. Stay with me.

elements.name: Meter glow-up
elements.description: Bring dead meters to life on cue

scenes.synopsis: Mara races the coast road on a motorbike toward the transmitter tower, headlight carving the dark.
scenes.scriptText:
EXT. CLIFF ROAD - NIGHT

A single headlight carves the black. MARA leans her bike hard through the switchbacks, the dead town falling away below.

scenes.synopsis: Mara and Eli argue, then rig her boat battery to the transmitter — the signal claws back to life.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. COASTAL RADIO STATION - NIGHT

MARA drops a marine battery on the desk. Sparks. ELI stares.

ELI
That'll fry the whole board.

MARA
Or it'll talk. Pick one.

scenes.synopsis: They climb to reset the dish; wind and height. A drone lifts to reveal the dark coast for miles.
scenes.scriptText:
EXT. TRANSMITTER TOWER BASE - NIGHT

Wind screams. ELI and MARA start up the gantry ladder. We rise with them — then keep rising, up past the dish, to reveal a coastline swallowed whole by the dark.

scenes.synopsis: INSERT — the drone holds high; one window, then a dozen, then a hundred flicker back on across the coast.
scenes.scriptText:
EXT. COAST - NIGHT (AERIAL INSERT)

From high above: one window glows. Then a dozen. Then a whole coastline of them, blooming back to life as the signal reaches home.

elements.name: Lights-return bloom
elements.description: CG windows bloom on across matte coast

scenes.synopsis: Dawn. The town is lit. Mara and Eli, wrecked and grinning, sign off the night broadcast.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. COASTAL RADIO STATION - DAWN

Grey light. Both of them slumped, filthy, alive. ELI leans to the mic.

ELI
That's the night shift. Get some sleep, coast. We've got you.

characterBible.name: Mara Chen
characterBible.description: Fisher's daughter. Fearless, blunt, keeps the boat and the town alive.

characterBible.name: Eli Okoro
characterBible.description: The last radio engineer. Stubborn, tender, married to the transmitter.

locations.name: Harbor Breakwater
locations.powerNotes: Genny only — no tie-in on the mole
locations.notes: Tide table pinned to call sheet. Night work.

banners.label: Split unit: aerial team on the ridge

tasks.title: Rig safe marine-battery jump gag (sc.4)
tasks.description: Practical spark, licensed pyro sign-off. Backup rig.

tasks.title: Motorbike prep + stunt rehearsal (cliff road)
tasks.description: Cornering passes with double; camera-bike tracking.

tasks.title: Drone flight plan + night waiver — aerial reveal
Pull EXT night permit — Cliff Road + Tower
Book 60kW genny + fuel for station backup

vfxShots.description: Coastline blackout — kill far-shore city glow
Dead meters glow up on cue
Vertical pull-up: sky replace + dark-coast extension

frequencyPlan.notes: Coordinate with county repeater — check scan on arrival.

cameraKits.name: B-Cam — ALEXA Mini LF + Ultra wides

drones.notes: Aerial reveal sc.5/5A. Night waiver filed with county.

notifications.title: PO-0002 needs review
notifications.body: SkyLine Aerial — drone day, AED 7,400. Awaiting accountant.

notifications.title: Cliff Road permit is blocked
notifications.body: EXT night permit blocked on traffic-management confirmation.

notifications.title: PO-0004 rejected
notifications.body: TideWater Marine — safety boat to be re-bid separately.

notifications.title: Company move added — Day 3
notifications.body: Cliff Road → Tower Base move at 22:30.

notifications.title: Daily digest ready
notifications.body: 4 shoot days scheduled · 2 POs pending · stunt rig blocked.

aiDigest.text: Day 1 (station interiors) is locked and camera-prepped, with the 60kW genny PO approved. The critical path runs through the night exteriors: the Cliff Road / Tower permit is blocked on traffic-management, which in turn holds the tower height-rig safety sign-off and the Day-3/4 aerial reveal. Two POs sit pending — the drone day and the stunt rigging — and TideWater's marine PO was kicked back to re-bid the safety boat. Budget is tracking against a AED 322,000 plan with VFX (blackout, aerial extension, lights-return bloom) still to spend.

roles.label: Administrator
roles.description: Full oversight of every project, department, users, roles, and AI.

roles.label: 1st AD / Scheduler
roles.description: Owns the shooting schedule, strip board, and DOOD.

roles.label: Accountant
roles.description: Manages the budget, POs, invoices, and petty cash.

roles.label: Camera / Technical
roles.description: Equipment manifests, kit builds, and prep checklists.

roles.label: VFX Supervisor
roles.description: Shot pipeline, vendor management, plate delivery.

roles.label: Art / Wardrobe / Props
roles.description: Element tracking, continuity, and set dressing.

roles.label: Cast Coordinator
roles.description: Cast schedules, DOOD, contracts, and call sheets.

projects.name: Salt & Static
projects.logline: A blackout swallows a coastal town; a radio engineer and a fisher's daughter fight through the night to keep one signal alive.

EXT. HARBOR BREAKWATER - DUSK

MARA CHEN, 24, weather-beaten, drags crates up the slick concrete. The string of harbor lights STUTTERS — then the entire coastline blacks out at once.

MARA
That's not a fuse.

Or it'll talk. Pick one.

From high above: one window glows. Then a dozen. Then a whole coastline of them, blooming back to life as the signal reaches home.

ELI
That's the night shift. Get some sleep, coast. We've got you.
